name: Sant Boi, Creu de la Missió, Colònia Güell – Circular Route
name_original: Sant Boi, Creu de la Missió, Colònia Güell – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Jogging routes around Sant Boi de Llobregat offer diverse landscapes, from riverine scenery to delta wetlands. The region is characterized by accessible trails that traverse varied terrain, including pine forests and open marshlands within the Llobregat Delta Natural Area. This area provides a blend of flat routes and paths with moderate elevation changes, catering to different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Sant Boi De Llobregat The most popular jogging route is Sant Boi, Creu de la Missió, Colònia Güell – Circular Route, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route features mostly accessible paths suitable for good fitness levels. Another top favourite among local runners is Golbes Fountain loop from Sant Boi, a difficult 8.5 miles (13.7 km) path. This route requires very good fitness and sure-footedness due to its challenging terrain and significant elevation gain. Local runners also love the Termes romanes loop from Molí Nou | Ciutat Cooperativa, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 3 minutes. Jogging around Sant Boi de Llobregat is defined by Llobregat Delta wetlands, riverine paths, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy, mostly flat paths to more challenging routes with notable ascents. The routes in Sant Boi de Llobregat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Sant Boi de Llobregat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: There are over 40 dedicated running routes around Sant Boi de Llobregat, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these trails over 900 times, giving them an average rating of 4.7 stars.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from flat, accessible paths through the &lt;strong&gt;Llobregat Delta Natural Area&lt;/strong&gt; to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains. You'll find a mix of riverine scenery, wetlands, pine forests, and varied surfaces suitable for both casual jogs and strenuous workouts.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: Yes, Sant Boi de Llobregat has options for beginners. The area features several easy, mostly flat paths, particularly within the Llobregat Delta and the &lt;strong&gt;Parc fluvial de Sant Boi de Llobregat&lt;/strong&gt;, which are ideal for those new to running or looking for a relaxed pace. Overall, 2 routes are classified as easy.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Sant Boi de Llobregat are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964363535/sant-boi-creu-de-la-missio-colonia-gueell-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Boi, Creu de la Missió, Colònia Güell – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular moderate option.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I might see while running?
A: While running in Sant Boi de Llobregat, you can explore the rich biodiversity of the &lt;strong&gt;Llobregat Delta Natural Area&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its migratory bird species. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/951471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellciuró Ruins&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6537362" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eramprunyà Castle&lt;/a&gt;. For a unique natural sight, consider routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2765506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Grotto in Parc de Torreblanca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous workout, there are difficult routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10450852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Golbes Fountain loop from Sant Boi&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.5-mile path requiring very good fitness and sure-footedness due to its challenging terrain. There are 14 routes classified as difficult in the area.

Q: Are the running routes in Sant Boi de Llobregat accessible by public transport?
A: Sant Boi de Llobregat is well-connected, and many running routes are accessible via public transport, especially those closer to urban centers or the Llobregat River Park. It's advisable to check specific route starting points for the nearest bus or train connections.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: The running routes in Sant Boi de Llobregat are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene river paths to the natural beauty of the delta wetlands, and the variety of trails catering to all fitness levels.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes offer a blend of nature and history. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33956093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Termes romanes loop from Molí Nou | Ciutat Cooperativa&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.2-mile trail that leads through varied landscapes and passes by the Roman Baths, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: Sant Boi de Llobregat offers good running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. However, the diverse landscapes, including the Llobregat Delta, can be enjoyed year-round, with cooler temperatures in winter and warmer conditions in summer.

Q: Are there options for longer distance running or trail running?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer distances or more extensive trail running, the region offers routes that can be combined or extended. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33942200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Golbes Fountain – Querol Cross loop from Molí Nou | Ciutat Cooperativa&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 11.7-mile route with significant elevation, providing a substantial workout.
tips: The second large metropolitan sewage treatment plant in Barcelona is Llobregat (the first is Besós). Activated in 2002 and expanded in 2006, the plant is built next to the mouth of the Llobregat river, next to the desalination plant of the same name.

The Llobregat is the second longest river in Catalonia, after the Ter. It empties into the Mediterranean south of the city of Barcelona. Its name could originate from an old Latin word meaning "dark", "sore" or "muddy", or from Rubricatus, "red".
Perfect meeting point to start routes or to wait for a straggler ;-)

The Colonia Güell crypt is a work by Antoni Gaudí, built between 1898 and 1914 as a religious building for his workers from Colonia Güell. Incredible place. Admission costs €9.50 but it is completely worth it. It perfectly portrays Gaudí's special universe.
